title: Kadal Asteroid Field

The '''Kadal Asteroid Field''' was located in the [[Bestal system/Legends|Bestal system]],<ref name="Polyhedron" /> a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Varada sector/Legends|Varada sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]] lay close to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bestal Three]] and contained [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] with significant deposits of [[aluminite]] and [[corax]] [[Ore/Legends|ores]].<ref name="Polyhedron" />

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], [[Alara Fax]], the head of the company [[Fax Ventures]], sought to exact vengeance upon her old rival, the [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation's]] Varada [[Sector/Legends|sector]] holdings' [[Top OpEx|top operating executive]] [[Tyanna Tymb]]. To that end, Fax studied the sector's [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] in search of business opportunities with which she could compete with Tymb. Discovering the Kadal Asteroid Field's ore deposits, Fax secured a contract that allowed her company to [[Mining/Legends|mine]] the asteroids and process the ore on Bestal Three. When Tymb later purchased [[Merchant/Legends|trade]] and resource rights for the Bestal system, she discovered the Fax Ventures' corporate rights to both the Kadal Asteroid Field and Bestal Three and subsequently became determined to acquire for the SoroSuub Corporation the rights to the entire system.<ref name="Polyhedron" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Kadal Asteroid Field was introduced in "[[Adversaries: Characters for the Star Wars: New Republic Campaign]]," a [[roleplaying game]] source article authored by [[Bill Slavicsek]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in [[Polyhedron 97|''Polyhedron'' 97]] in [[July]] [[1994]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Polyhedron" /> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Bestal system, and therefore the asteroid field, in grid square I-19.<ref name="TEA Bestal" />
